original_image_tags: Aged, yellowish paper document, likely the back of a form or cover sheet. The page shows significant bleed-through text from the front side, which is largely illegible but appears to be typed bureaucratic correspondence. The page is heavily stamped with various routing and receipt dates. Top right corner is torn and missing. Top left has a large hole punch. Right edge has a jagged tear.

elements.description: The back of a document featuring multiple ink stamps and faded reverse-side text showing through the thin paper. Visible stamps include: 'REC'D ESPIONAGE - NAT. INTL.' dated 'MAR 18 2 03 PM '58'; 'RECEIVED BOARDMAN' dated 'MAR 14 4 01 PM'; 'REC'D MAIL ROOM FBI' dated 'MAR 11 11 52 AM '58'; 'REC'D BELMONT FBI - JUSTICE' dated 'Mar 13 9 31 AM '58' and 'Mar 14 3 39 PM '58'; 'REC'D BELMONT FBI - JUSTICE' dated 'Mar 14 10 06 AM '58'. There is also a faint red stamp in the center and a red stamp at the bottom reading 'MAR 31 1958'. Punch holes and a tear are visible at the top.

judge.reason: Description B provides extensive, specific verbatim stamp text and dates that Description A claims are 'largely illegible', indicating at least one side is fabricating detailed visual content the other cannot confirm.
